sourceRefs.fallbackText: Dynamic web applications also need static files. That’s usually where the CSS and JavaScript files are coming from. Ideally your web server is configured to serve them for you, but during development Flask can do that as well. Just create a folder called static in your package or next to your module and it will be available at /static on the application.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: When returning HTML (the default response type in Flask), any user-provided values rendered in the output must be escaped to protect from injection attacks. HTML templates rendered with Jinja, introduced later, will do this automatically.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: For web applications it’s crucial to react to the data a client sends to the server. In Flask this information is provided by the global request object.
